Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `account`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. params.ringsDID: The Rings DID (bytes32) that should receive the                  equivalent tokens on the Rings network.

depositToken(address,uint256,bytes32).details: The caller must have approved this contract for `amount` tokens.      Uses SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom for safe interaction.      Emits {Deposited}.

params.amount: Number of tokens to deposit (in smallest unit).
params.ringsDID: The Rings DID that should receive the equivalent                  tokens on the Rings network.
params.token: Address of the ERC20 token contract.

returns._0: Remaining capacity in wei.  Resets when a new 24-hour         window begins.

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

returns._0: True if a deposit with this nonce exists.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `account`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

unpause().details: Only callable by DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (more restrictive than pause).

updateRateLimits(uint256,uint256).details: Only callable by GUARDIAN_ROLE.  The per-tx limit must not      exceed the daily limit.

updateSyncCommittee(bytes32,uint64,bytes.details: The new slot must be strictly greater than the current one to      prevent replay.  The ZK proof must verify against the on-chain      Groth16Verifier.

params.proof: ZK proof that the committee rotation is valid.
params.publicInputs: Public inputs for the rotation proof.
params.slot: Rings slot number the new committee covers.

withdraw(address,bytes32,uint256,uint256.details: Checks: proof validity → replay protection → rate limits.      Effects: record withdrawal, update daily volume.      Interactions: transfer ETH last (CEI pattern).

params.publicInputs: Public inputs for the proof (must encode                      recipient, ringsDID, amount, nonce).
params.recipient: Ethereum address to receive the ETH.
params.ringsDID: Rings DID that initiated the withdrawal.

withdrawToken(address,address,bytes32,ui.details: The bridge must hold BRIDGE_ROLE on the BridgedToken contract.

params.bridgedToken: Address of the BridgedToken contract to mint.
params.recipient: Ethereum address to receive the tokens.
params.ringsDID: Rings DID that initiated the withdrawal.

dailyLimit().notice: Maximum total withdrawal volume per 24-hour window (in wei).

dailyVolume().notice: Cumulative withdrawal volume in the current daily window.

deposit(bytes32).notice: Deposit native ETH into the bridge.

depositToken(address,uint256,bytes32).notice: Deposit ERC20 tokens into the bridge.

emergencyPause().notice: Emergency pause — halts all deposits and withdrawals.

getDepositInfo(uint256).notice: Retrieve a deposit record by nonce.

getRemainingDailyLimit().notice: Get the remaining daily withdrawal capacity.

getWithdrawalInfo(uint256).notice: Retrieve a withdrawal record by nonce.

isDepositProcessed(uint256).notice: Check whether a given deposit nonce has been recorded.

lastResetTimestamp().notice: Timestamp at which the current daily window started.

latestVerifiedSlot().notice: Slot number of the latest verified Rings consensus state.

processedWithdrawals(uint256).notice: Replay protection: withdrawal nonce → processed flag.

syncCommitteeRoot().notice: Current sync committee root (Poseidon hash of the committee).

unpause().notice: Unpause the bridge after an emergency.

updateSyncCommittee(bytes32,uint64,bytes.notice: Update the Rings sync committee root with a new verified         committee.

verifier().notice: Address of the deployed Groth16Verifier contract.

withdraw(address,bytes32,uint256,uint256.notice: Withdraw native ETH from the bridge after ZK proof         verification.

withdrawToken(address,address,bytes32,ui.notice: Withdraw bridged ERC20 tokens.  Instead of releasing locked         tokens, this mints new BridgedTokens to the recipient.
